commit.message:
fix(ci): pass RELEASE_TOKEN to checkout for git push auth

The checkout step configures git credentials. Without passing the PAT
here, git push still uses the default GITHUB_TOKEN which cannot
bypass branch protection.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: ha-homekit-heatercooler

commit.message:
fix(ci): use RELEASE_TOKEN for semantic-release push

GITHUB_TOKEN cannot bypass branch protection required status checks.
Use a fine-grained PAT (RELEASE_TOKEN) with admin permissions instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Move multiplayerMode to store; fix persistent queue toggle

The 'Queue for Run' button appeared to do nothing because
multiplayerMode was local useState(false) — it reset to false every
time the user navigated away from and back to the Deploy tab, silently
reverting the button to 'Deploy'. Clicking 'Deploy' starts a solo run
immediately with no queue feedback, which looked like nothing happened.

- multiplayerStore: add multiplayerMode boolean + setMultiplayerMode()
  so the toggle persists across tab switches
- DeploymentScreen: source multiplayerMode from the store instead of
  local state; update toggle handler to use the store setter
- DeploymentScreen: also pull status from store; only show the error
  banner for genuine errors (status=error or 'unable' messages), not
  the 'Multiplayer foundation connected.' success message

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix multiplayer run loop: move cleanup to AppShell

The core bug: endMultiplayerRun() was only called inside
ActiveRunPanel, which only renders on the Overview screen. If the
player was on any other tab when the run ended, cleanup never fired,
queueState stayed 'matched', and visiting Deploy triggered a new run.

- AppShell: add global useEffect watching activeRun — when a run ends
  while activeRunSession is set, call endMultiplayerRun() and refresh
  MMR regardless of which screen is active
- ActiveRunPanel: remove the duplicate run-end observer (now redundant)
- DeploymentScreen: add startedSessionRef to track which sessionId
  we've already launched — prevents re-firing for the same session
  even if queueState hasn't cleared yet

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix infinite run loop after multiplayer match ends

- multiplayerStore: endMultiplayerRun now clears queueState (was
  leaving status='matched' forever, causing re-trigger on next mount)
- DeploymentScreen: guard useEffect against firing if activeRun is
  already set — belt-and-suspenders against future stale queueState

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add bot players and queue timeout (90s)

- botStore.ts: 50 sci-fi runner names, createBotProfile(mmr) with +-50
  MMR variance, isBot(userId) helper
- queueStore.ts: queuedAt timestamp on every entry
- runSessionStore.ts: startBotMovement() advances bot 1 room every
  8-15s via updatePosition(), clearing on endSession(); botIntervals
  tracked per-session
- matchmaker.ts: second pass per tick — entries waiting >90s get
  silently matched with a bot; enrichRunnerNames() extracted as helper
- runs.ts: isBot() guards on Supabase MMR writes and encounter inserts

Players never know they were matched with a bot.
BOT_TIMEOUT_MS = 90_000 (easy to tune in matchmaker.ts)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add bot players and 90s queue timeout

- botStore.ts: pool of 50 sci-fi runner names, createBotProfile(mmr)
  returns a fake profile with ±50 MMR variance; isBot() helper
- queueStore.ts: add queuedAt timestamp to queue entries
- runSessionStore.ts: startBotMovement() advances bot 1 room every
  8-15s (randomised), triggering real zone overlap/PvP checks;
  endSession() clears bot timers; SECTOR_ROOMS map for room limits
- matchmaker.ts: pass 2 after real pairing — entries waiting >90s
  get silently matched with a bot (BOT_TIMEOUT_MS = 90_000);
  extracted enrichRunnerNames() helper; bot sessions log clearly
- runs.ts: skip Supabase MMR/encounter persist for bot userIds;
  encounter record uses real player IDs when one side is a bot

Players are never informed they matched with a bot.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(v7.5.5.1): BUG-058 aggregator backoff bypass, BUG-059 https:// validator gap (#65)

* fix(v7.5.5.1): BUG-058 backoff for never-reached satellites, BUG-059 reject https:// URLs

BUG-058: When a satellite was never successfully polled, last_*_fetch
stayed at 0 and the "due" check always returned true, bypassing the
300-second unreachable backoff. On a C3 with an offline satellite this
caused 15 seconds of blocking per 5-second loop. Fix: seed last_*_fetch
to `now` on first failure so the interval timer starts counting.

BUG-059: validate_aggregator_config() accepted https:// base_url values
but fetch_to_buffer() only supports http://. An https satellite URL
would pass validation but be permanently unreachable at runtime. Fix:
reject https:// with a clear error message explaining it's not yet
supported.

Adds BUG-058, BUG-059, LESSON-OPS-069 to bugs-and-lessons-learned.md.

Co-authored-by: GCV-Sleeper-Service <45667495+GCV-Sleeper-Service@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/GCV-Sleeper-Service/ESP32-GW-multi-sensor/sessions/709b8385-2458-42a3-ace5-21331bbd38d8

* fix(BUG-058): move timestamp seeding inside failures >= 3 threshold

Per Codex review: seeding last_*_fetch on every failure suppresses
retries for 5 minutes after a single transient boot-time failure,
defeating the 3-failure threshold designed for boot-order tolerance.

Move the seeding inside the `if (failures >= 3)` block so it only
activates when the satellite is actually declared unreachable.

Behavior by failure count:
- Failures 1-2: retry at normal frequency (timestamps stay 0, due=true)
- Failure 3+: satellite marked unreachable, timestamps seeded, 300s backoff

Co-authored-by: GCV-Sleeper-Service <45667495+GCV-Sleeper-Service@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/GCV-Sleeper-Service/ESP32-GW-multi-sensor/sessions/1dbb4154-e995-4929-b4a3-929f0f9d0c45

* fix(BUG-058): use monotonic uptime for interval tracking, not wall-clock

Per Codex review: ::time(nullptr) returns 0 before SNTP sync, making
the BUG-058 backoff seeding a no-op (last_*_fetch = 0 is unchanged).

Split `now` into:
- uptime_s: esp_timer_get_time()/1e6 — monotonic, for interval math
- epoch_now: ::time(nullptr) — wall-clock, for last_seen_epoch only

uptime_s is always nonzero after the 10s boot delay, so backoff
seeding and interval comparisons work regardless of SNTP state.

Co-authored-by: GCV-Sleeper-Service <45667495+GCV-Sleeper-Service@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/GCV-Sleeper-Service/ESP32-GW-multi-sensor/sessions/1da05d81-346e-4244-89e6-030b2411ecc7

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: GCV-Sleeper-Service <45667495+GCV-Sleeper-Service@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
v7.5.5.1: Aggregator polling task (#64)

* v7.5.5.1: aggregator polling task — SatelliteCache, mutex, fetch_to_buffer, aggregator_poll_task

Co-authored-by: GCV-Sleeper-Service <45667495+GCV-Sleeper-Service@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/GCV-Sleeper-Service/ESP32-GW-multi-sensor/sessions/42837805-539e-43d1-8a7c-5faffd3564f0

* fix(v7.5.5.1): post-review corrections — stack size, priority, session log

- xTaskCreate stack: 6144 → 10240 (ESP HTTP client needs ~3KB on stack)
- xTaskCreate priority: tskIDLE_PRIORITY+1 → +2 (prevent starvation)
- Create session log Docs/session-log-2026-03-22-v7.5.5.1.md
- Add Instruction Compliance Output to PR description

Co-authored-by: GCV-Sleeper-Service <45667495+GCV-Sleeper-Service@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/GCV-Sleeper-Service/ESP32-GW-multi-sensor/sessions/f7dbe8f0-42a7-4600-88c8-d23ef5ccde2a

* fix(v7.5.5.1): epoch timestamps, unreachable back-off, recovery logging

- Replace esp_timer_get_time()/1e6 with ::time(nullptr) for correct
  Unix epoch semantics (required for v7.5.5.2 API responses)
- Add 300s back-off interval for unreachable satellites (saves CPU
  on C3 single-core; prevents 15s blocking per cycle on dead hosts)
- Add "recovered" log message on unreachable -> reachable transition
  (observability for device testing per §11)
- Update session log with new post-review corrections

Co-authored-by: GCV-Sleeper-Service <45667495+GCV-Sleeper-Service@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/GCV-Sleeper-Service/ESP32-GW-multi-sensor/sessions/c58b60d4-5150-4cea-b79f-69eb0fa8275c

* fix: replace esp_http_client with raw lwIP socket HTTP to fix CI compile

esp_http_client.h is not in ESPHome's IDF main component PRIV_REQUIRES
because ESPHome doesn't use that component internally. lwip/sockets.h
is already in PRIV_REQUIRES via the network stack.

New fetch_to_buffer() uses raw HTTP/1.0 GET over BSD sockets:
- Parses http://host[:port]/path URLs
- lwip_getaddrinfo() for DNS (consistent with PingAdapter)
- 5s SO_RCVTIMEO/SO_SNDTIMEO timeouts
- HTTP/1.0 (no chunked encoding)
- Headers read into 512-byte stack buffer until \r\n\r\n
- Body read directly into caller's buffer (no extra copy)

Also corrects changelog and session log to reflect the actual
lwIP socket implementation (not esp_http_client) and correct
xTaskCreate parameters (stack 10240, priority +2).

Co-authored-by: GCV-Sleeper-Service <45667495+GCV-Sleeper-Service@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/GCV-Sleeper-Service/ESP32-GW-multi-sensor/sessions/f961022a-052e-4bf1-bf1e-ba24177a731c

* fix(v7.5.5.1): replace BSD socket aliases with lwip_* to fix esphome::socket namespace collision

ESPHome defines `namespace esphome::socket` which makes bare `socket()`,
`connect()`, `close()` etc. ambiguous when lwip/sockets.h is also
included. Replace all BSD-compat aliases in fetch_to_buffer() with their
lwip_*() prefixed equivalents (lwip_socket, lwip_connect, lwip_send,
lwip_recv, lwip_close, lwip_setsockopt). These are the actual lwIP
functions — the unprefixed names are inline wrappers.

Matches the existing pattern used by PingAdapter (lwip_getaddrinfo,
lwip_freeaddrinfo).

Adds BUG-057 and LESSON-OPS-068 to bugs-and-lessons-learned.md.

Fixes CI compilation failure in PR #64.

Co-authored-by: GCV-Sleeper-Service <45667495+GCV-Sleeper-Service@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/GCV-Sleeper-Service/ESP32-GW-multi-sensor/sessions/6025704f-95bc-4d74-95eb-3492408ed2e8

---------

Co-authored-by: GCV-Sleeper-Service <45667495+GCV-Sleeper-Service@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve toISOString error on session RPE save

Root cause: updatedAt columns had mode:'date' but no defaultNow(),
returning null on first insert. SuperJSON tried to serialize the
Date-typed null. Also sessionType passed null (not undefined) to
Zod .optional() field.

Fixes:
- Add .defaultNow() to all 6 updatedAt columns
- Change $onUpdateFn to return new Date() (JS value, not sql template)
- Convert sessionType null → undefined with ?? operator
- Remove unused sql import from drizzle-orm

Switch to Railpack builder; remove nixpacks.toml

Railpack auto-detects Bun, no provider hint needed.

Remove bun.lock: version mismatch with Railway's Bun 1.2.15

nixpacks.toml providers=[bun] is sufficient for Bun detection.
The lockfile was generated with Bun 1.3.11 locally which causes
frozen-lockfile errors on Railway's older Bun version.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add bun.lock so NIXPACKS auto-detects Bun runtime

Bun 1.2+ uses bun.lock (text) instead of bun.lockb (binary).
Combined with nixpacks.toml providers=[bun], this ensures Railway
installs and uses Bun for builds and the server start command.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add nixpacks.toml to force Bun provider on Railway

NIXPACKS defaults to Node when package-lock.json is present.
This tells it to use the Bun provider so the start command
(bun server/dist/server/src/index.js) works correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Remove _redirects: wrangler.jsonc handles SPA routing via not_found_handling

The _redirects file conflicts with the Workers static assets config.
SPA fallback is already handled by wrangler.jsonc's
assets.not_found_handling = 'single-page-application'.

commit.message:
Add hosting config: Cloudflare Pages + Railway + GitHub Actions CI/CD

- public/_redirects: SPA fallback route for Cloudflare Pages
- railway.json: build + start commands for Railway deployment
- .github/workflows/deploy.yml: build on PR, deploy on push to main
  - client -> Cloudflare Pages via wrangler-action
  - server -> Railway via railway CLI
- server/src/app.ts: read ALLOWED_ORIGIN env var for CORS lockdown in prod
  (unset = permissive true for local dev)
- .env.example: documented ALLOWED_ORIGIN, PORT, HOST

Required GitHub secrets to configure:
  VITE_SUPABASE_URL, VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, VITE_MULTIPLAYER_API_URL
  CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN, CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID
  RAILWAY_TOKEN
  SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY (set in Railway dashboard)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add co-op run system: matchmaking, zone PvP, live leaderboard

- Matchmaking worker (5s tick) pairs players in same sector within ±300 MMR
- Run sessions: shared server-side state, position sync every 2s during runs
- Zone overlap detection (20% encounter chance) triggers server-authoritative PvP
- PvP resolution: MMR-weighted 60/40, ±25 MMR delta, +10 underdog bonus
- PvP events pushed via WebSocket (/ws/run/:sessionId) to both players
- MMR updates and encounter records persisted to Supabase after PvP
- Live leaderboard: real Supabase queries (global/regional top-10 by MMR)
- DeploymentScreen: Co-op mode toggle (requires auth), auto-starts run on match
- ActiveRunPanel: position sync, PvP encounter/outcome overlay during run
- Multiplayer tab: updated Queue Status panel, co-op tip

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add username selection on first login

Instead of auto-generating runner names from OAuth metadata, new users now
choose their own username before accessing multiplayer features.

Flow:
  1. User signs in via OAuth
  2. GET /api/auth/profile returns needsSetup: true (no profile row yet)
  3. authStore transitions to 'setup-required' status
  4. MultiplayerScreen shows UsernameSetupCard inline
  5. User submits a name → POST /api/auth/setup creates the profile row
  6. authStore transitions to 'authenticated' with the new profile

Username rules (enforced client + server):
  - 3–20 characters, letters/numbers/underscores only
  - Case-insensitive uniqueness (unique index on lower(runner_name))
  - Duplicate names return a 409 with a clear message

Changes:
  - supabase/migrations/002_username_unique.sql: unique index + drop default
  - shared/src/multiplayer.ts: needsSetup field on AuthProfileResponse;
    new UsernameSetupRequest/Response schemas
  - server/src/routes/auth.ts: stop auto-creating profile; return needsSetup:
    true; add POST /api/auth/setup
  - server/src/shared.ts: export new schemas
  - server/src/data/sampleData.ts: add needsSetup: false, remove github
  - src/lib/multiplayerApi.ts: add callSetupUsername()
  - src/store/authStore.ts: setup-required status + completeSetup() action
  - src/components/screens/MultiplayerScreen.tsx: UsernameSetupCard component

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: Docker CVE remediation - eliminate tsx from prod, patch nginx base

- Rewrite server/Dockerfile.prod: multi-stage esbuild compile, plain node runtime
  - Eliminates tsx (Go binary source of 3 critical CVEs: CVE-2025-68121, CVE-2024-24790, CVE-2025-22871)
  - Image size: 2.7GB -> 600MB (77% reduction)
  - ZK circuit symlink for esbuild path resolution
- Upgrade all 6 Dockerfiles from node:20 to node:22
- Patch client/Dockerfile.prod: nginx:1-alpine + apk upgrade (CVE-2026-32767 expat fix)
- Add npm overrides for underscore, minimatch, basic-ftp CVEs
- Add nanoid, node-fetch as explicit prod dependencies
- npm audit: 0 critical, 0 high, 0 moderate (only 15 low from ZK libs)
- Server v2.6.0 deployed and verified (API routes, ZK proofs, health all working)
- Frontend v2.1.25 deployed with patched nginx base

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Switch server runtime to Bun

- Replace 'tsx watch' with 'bun --hot server/src/index.ts'
- Remove tsx devDependency (Bun runs TypeScript natively)
- .env.local is loaded automatically by Bun (no --env-file flag needed)
- --hot gives module-level hot reload vs full process restart
- Verified: health endpoint responds correctly under Bun 1.3.11

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix server env loading: use node --env-file=.env.local

Replace 'tsx watch' with 'node --env-file=.env.local --watch --import tsx/esm'
so .env.local is loaded into process.env before any modules are evaluated.
Requires Node 20.6+ (project is on Node 22).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Remove GitHub OAuth provider (Google and Discord only)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Warm the token highlight palette

Rebalance the token highlight colors away from blue-heavy tones so the page reads less blue overall while keeping the tokenization view visually distinct.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
